pr.title: Match lb_80011AC4 with new LightList type
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Introduced a typed SceneDesc light-list representation and used it to fully match lb_80011AC4. The PR replaces ad hoc HSD_LightDesc**/UNK_T/fake node structs with LightList and LightList** across library, scene, fighter, menu, title, and ground code. lb_80011AC4 now constructs an HSD_LObj linked list from a NULL-terminated LightList** array, loads each HSD_LightDesc, attaches the first HSD_LightAnim when present, and links the resulting HSD_LObj nodes. The decomp.dev report confirms lb_80011AC4 became a new 100% match worth 176 bytes and Ground_801C466C improved from 82.09% to 83.50%.
what_changed: Added a forward declaration for struct LightList in src/melee/sc/forward.h.
Named the scene light-list entry struct as struct LightList in src/melee/sc/types.h, with fields HSD_LightDesc* desc and HSD_LightAnim** anims.
Changed SceneDesc lights-related users from HSD_LightDesc** or UNK_T to LightList** where the data is a NULL-terminated array of pointers to LightList entries.
Implemented lb_80011AC4(LightList** list) in src/melee/lb/lb_00F9.c instead of leaving it as a TODO/unknown prototype.
Updated lb_80011AC4 declarations in lb_00F9.h and lb_00F9.dox from UNK_T/HSD_GObj* style placeholders to HSD_LObj* lb_80011AC4(LightList**).
Replaced fake local node1_t/node2_t structs in ftCo_09F4.c with a static LightList node1 and a static LightList* node2[] array, allowing ftCo_8009F578 to call lb_80011AC4(node2) without a cast.
Updated ground light-return paths and locals, including Ground_801C466C_inline and Ground_801C49B4, to return LightList**.
Adjusted Ground_801C466C field usage from next/class_name assumptions to the new LightList anims field, checking (*var_r28_2)->anims and passing (*var_r27_2)->anims[0] to Ground_801C43C4.
Changed gmTitle_804D670C and MenMain_lights to LightList**.
smart_moves: The core matching move was to identify that the scene light payload was not a raw HSD_LightDesc** but a list node pairing a light descriptor with optional light animations.
By promoting the anonymous SceneDesc light entry to a named LightList type, the PR removed casts and fake node structs while also improving type propagation through unrelated callers.
The ftCo_09F4 cleanup demonstrates a reusable pattern: replace locally invented fake resource structs with the shared archive/scene type once the format is understood.

The implementation of lb_80011AC4 mirrors the data shape directly: iterate over a NULL-terminated LightList** array, load each desc with HSD_LObjLoadDesc, optionally add anims[0], then link HSD_LObj::next.

In Ground_801C466C_inline, introducing a named UnkArchiveStruct* archive local made a previously suspicious Ground_801C20E0(0, grDatFiles_801C6330(i)->...) expression type-correct and helped matching.
decomp_lessons: When a function consumes archive scene data, inspect the owning scene struct before settling for HSD_* pointer aliases; the correct match may require naming the intermediate resource-list node.
A NULL-terminated array of pointers to resource entries can decompile more cleanly as Type** than as a casted descriptor pointer array.
Typed headers can unlock matches across multiple systems because scene archive fields are shared by fighter setup, ground/stage loading, menus, and title screens.
Fake local structs are useful temporary scaffolding, but once their shape matches a shared resource format they should be collapsed into the canonical project type.
Light animation data belongs beside light descriptors in LightList::anims; future light-loading code should not treat the second field as generic UNK_T unless evidence is missing.
Propagating one precise type through function prototypes, globals, and static arrays can remove casts and reveal field accesses that better match original code generation.
naming_conventions: Use LightList for the scene light-entry struct containing desc and anims.
Use LightList** for pointers to NULL-terminated arrays of LightList* entries, including SceneDesc::lights and light-loading globals.
Use desc for HSD_LightDesc* inside LightList.
Use anims for HSD_LightAnim** inside LightList.
Keep the existing address-based function names lb_80011AC4, Ground_801C466C_inline, and Ground_801C49B4 where no semantic names were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matched lb_80011AC4 loop uses while (*list != NULL) and increments list++ over a pointer array.
HSD_LObjLoadDesc((*list)->desc) produces each light object from the LightList descriptor field.
The optional animation path loads temp_r4 = (*list)->anims and calls HSD_LObjAddAnimAll(curr, temp_r4[0]) only when anims is non-NULL.
The linked-list construction preserves a prev pointer, assigns first for the initial node, and links later nodes through prev->next = curr.
Leaving the apparently redundant nested if (prev == NULL) __assert("lobj.h", 0x136, "lobj") inside the prev != NULL branch was part of the matched decomp output in the diff.
Changing ftCo_09F4 static data from a wrapper struct with x0/x4 to LightList plus LightList*[] removed a forced cast at the lb_80011AC4 call site.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported 1 new match: main/melee/lb/lb_00F9 lb_80011AC4, +176 bytes, 0.00% to 100.00%.
The decomp.dev bot also reported Ground_801C466C improved by +12 bytes, from 82.09% to 83.50%.
follow_up_queries: Find other HSD_LightDesc** or UNK_T light globals that should become LightList**.
Search for remaining fake node structs around scene resource lists that can be replaced by named SceneDesc subtypes.
Audit other SceneDesc arrays for descriptor-plus-animation patterns similar to LightList.
Investigate whether Ground_801C466C can be further improved now that LightList::anims has a concrete type.
Search for callers of lb_80011AC4 to ensure they all pass LightList** without casts.
